author: @mouldykev
text:
I am loving this one Rob, not only does this completely interest me but i totally get Steam punk to and have done so for years.
However i need to ask about the drive motor, where or what was it from.

Regards kev

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: Coming along very well indeed. The cowl construction is brilliant. Could I ask what has happened to the Wind Turbine project, I think a lot of people were following closely.

author: @edkoetsier339
text: Hi Rob. Last time I'm asking. If you don't answer, I'll assume you don't want to. Which would be odd because I'm a paying member. In one of the Bagdad videos, you mentioned you made your own carbon felts and had already made a video on how to do it. Would you please point me to it. I cannot find in Members or General. Cheers

author: @BushImports
text: I was wondering if you were really talented at forming sheet metal, it looks nicely done. Thanks for sharing that.

author: @tobiwheeler
text: When will we see the completed wind turbine or the gassifire running something????????????????????

author: @chuxxsss
text: Afternoon Rob, another wonderful vlog mate.

author: @charlesdickens6706
text: ....and the twizzy alongside gets our attention. I don't understand why Renault had to make it so complicated to mess with. All that an electric road vehicle needs to be is simply a slight improvement of a golf buggy. I ain't a fan of glued together tinsel Tesla shiny wedges .

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: Ok I got a two part question. I'm wondering why you didn't use a heat gun to just bend the shape out of a perfect cutout, being that it was made of PVC. I have bent PVC in this way on a lot of projects... My second question is, towards the end of this video at the back of the room there is this steel yellow looking contraption on the back counter, what is it? My guess was a press or break of some kind. I would like to see it closer and in action if possible, it looks very interestingly built. Cheers mate

author: @wongowonga
text: Rob, have you ever though about taking a 'celebrity name'? I suggest Mad Max II

author: @karlmyers6518
text:
All I need to know is will the battery last 110 miles from yours to mine. 
I had an awesome day building dishwashers today.

author: @javiermorton7170
text: Funny that a whole other cool topic almost didnt get a video made. It is  a compliment to how many really cool things you have going on.

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: Lol would it have enough power to fit a sidecar? I'd say it looks slightly e-type more than wrong trousers?

author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
text: have you thought about adding a basket or small cargo box, maybe over the back wheel?   might be handy.

author: @NorthernKitty
text: Wondering if you used the body filler from Luke's video on "DIY Glass Body Filler"?  That would make things kind of "full circle" on your respective projects. 😊  Kind of neat to see the crossover between the two of you on this build - I just watched Luke's kick scooter, and really impressed with the job he's done!  Can't wait to see the two of you "racing around" in the lot, LOL!!!  😋
